message:
feat(core): Enhance codon utilities with IUPAC ambiguity code support

translateCodon():
- Change unknown codon fallback from '*' to 'X'
- 'X' is the standard IUPAC single-letter code for unknown amino acid
- '*' is reserved for stop codons (UAA, UAG, UGA)
- Prevents confusion between stop codons and translation failures

reverseComplement():
- Add full IUPAC ambiguity code complement mapping:
  - R↔Y: Purine (A/G) ↔ Pyrimidine (T/C)
  - S↔S: Strong (G/C) self-complementary
  - W↔W: Weak (A/T) self-complementary
  - K↔M: Keto (G/T) ↔ Amino (A/C)
  - B↔V: Not-A (C/G/T) ↔ Not-T (A/C/G)
  - D↔H: Not-C (A/G/T) ↔ Not-G (A/C/T)
  - H↔D: Not-G (A/C/T) ↔ Not-C (A/G/T)
  - V↔B: Not-T (A/C/G) ↔ Not-A (C/G/T)
- Handles both uppercase and lowercase variants
- Unknown characters passed through unchanged (defensive)

These changes enable proper handling of:
- Degenerate primers with ambiguity codes
- Consensus sequences with uncertainty
- Low-quality regions marked with IUPAC codes
- Comparative genomics across divergent sequences

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
